acuteCalfInjuryJun2026.priority: Self-screen on 6/23 was reassuring (see selfScreenJun23), so urgency is lowered. A professional (physio/sports-med) assessment is still recommended for grading and rehab progression, but is no longer time-critical. Re-escalate urgently if DVT signs develop (swollen, warm, red, or one-sided calf) or pain becomes disproportionate. Coaching note, not a diagnosis.
acuteCalfInjuryJun2026.presentation: Sun 6/21 AM basketball: minor right-calf pull played through, then acute severe left-calf pain ('shot with a lacrosse ball'), difficulty bearing weight since. Classic acute medial gastrocnemius strain ('tennis leg'), at least Grade II given the can't-weight-bear onset.
acuteCalfInjuryJun2026.selfScreenJun23: Later 6/23: no swelling or bruising, calf symmetric, and the Thompson/Simmonds calf-squeeze test normal and symmetric (foot plantarflexes both sides). Complete Achilles rupture now much less likely (Boyd 2015 PMID 26622031); DVT lower probability given no swelling/asymmetry (Wells 1997 PMID 9428249). Working dx: acute medial gastrocnemius strain, likely Grade I-II.

ruleOut.test: Achilles tendon rupture - Thompson/Simmonds calf-squeeze test
ruleOut.reason: THE can't-miss diagnosis. Rupture patients can still walk and weakly plantarflex and may have no palpable gap, so it is missed unless the squeeze test is done. A radiological 'partial tear' is treated as complete until proven otherwise (Boyd 2015 PMID 26622031; Maffulli 1998 PMID 9548122, squeeze-test sensitivity 0.96).

ruleOut.test: DVT assessment - Wells score +/- Doppler ultrasound
ruleOut.reason: DVT is found in ~10% of tennis-leg presentations and can mimic or be masked by a strain. Red flags: disproportionate unilateral swelling, warmth, redness, calf >3 cm larger than the other side (Delgado 2002 PMID 12091669; Wells 1997 PMID 9428249).

If pain is out of proportion to exam with a tense/firm compartment and pain on passive stretch, seek emergency care - these are early signs; pulselessness/pallor are late (Pechar & Lyons 2016, DOI 10.1016/j.nurpra.2015.10.013).

acuteCalfInjuryJun2026.imaging: Ultrasound (first-line) or MRI to confirm location, grade, and aponeurotic/intramuscular-tendon involvement, which predicts a longer return to sport (Green 2020 PMID 33030961; Meek 2022 DOI 10.2106/JBJS.RVW.21.00183).
acuteCalfInjuryJun2026.reference: Full evidence + phased rehab in knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md.

clinicalWorkupAsks.nextPhysicianVisit: Request the following labs together to cover multiple coaching questions:

required.reason: Monitor iron balance given Double Reds donation 2-3x/year + 65mg daily iron supplementation. Target 30-200 ng/mL. Below 30 = deficient despite supplement; above 200 = overloaded, reduce dose.
Complete iron workup alongside ferritin. Detects anemia or overload context.

recommended.reason: Baseline given current 5g/day psyllium (under the 10-15g dose needed for measurable LDL effect). If LDL elevated, bump psyllium to 10g/day for 8-12 weeks and recheck. Also relevant for fish oil dose review (currently 980mg EPA+DHA, sub-therapeutic for lipid effects).
One-time baseline. San Diego + outdoor play = likely sufficient, but a data point is cheap. Target 30-60 ng/mL.

recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.reason: Hyperthyroidism is one of the most common drivers of elevated SHBG. Jul 2025 panel showed SHBG 83 (upper limit 50). Rule out thyroid driver before attributing high SHBG to genetic/idiopathic.
Liver synthesizes SHBG. Elevated enzymes or altered liver function can increase SHBG. Rule out liver-driven elevation.
Low-insulin states elevate SHBG. Given mixed metabolic picture, worth characterizing insulin sensitivity.

recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.test: Repeat T panel in 3-6 months with free T + SHBG + Estradiol + Prolactin
recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.reason: Confirm the elevated-SHBG pattern is persistent, not a one-time reading. Same collection protocol (morning, fasted, pre-exercise, not within 2-3 weeks of Power Red donation).

testosteroneHistory.notes: Reference range 40s: 264-916 ng/dL. Within normal. Collection time unknown - if drawn afternoon, AM equivalent would have been higher since T is ~50% lower PM vs AM.
Optimal morning collection. Above age-40 average (~500-600 ng/dL). Free T and SHBG not drawn - limited interpretation without them (see 2025-07 panel showing high-SHBG pattern).

testosteroneHistory.freeTestosteroneFlag: low-normal (at floor, 3.4 pg/mL above deficiency threshold)
testosteroneHistory.notes: KEY FINDING: elevated SHBG (83 vs upper limit 50) is pulling total T out of circulation. Total T 811 is upper-normal but free/bioavailable T are at the BOTTOM of range. This is a 'functionally low-T presentation' despite robust total T. Technically does not meet Endocrine Society 2018 hypogonadism criteria (free T above floor, no reported symptoms). Borderline high estradiol tracks with high total T (aromatization). Prolactin normal rules out pituitary cause. Next step: understand SHBG driver - thyroid (TSH + free T4), liver (AST/ALT/bilirubin), metabolic (fasting insulin / HbA1c). Source: Quest Diagnostics ZD478125F Men's Hormone Panel Expanded, ordering physician MARKS, Lianne, PWN Health.

nutritionGoals.primary: Steady fat loss preserving lean mass, strength, and game performance
nutritionGoals.rationale: Apr 20 body-comp overview showed +4.7 lbs fat / +1.9 lbs lean over 6 weeks. Initial 2-day/week deficit-at-BMR approach (set Apr 20 AM) was recalibrated later that day based on Helms 2014 and Aragon 2017 ISSN evidence: (1) rest-day target at BMR was more aggressive than needed, (2) intermittent restriction is not superior to daily small deficits per ISSN 2017, (3) weekly deficit was below the Helms 0.5%/wk floor anyway. Revised to moderate-deficit across 3 non-heavy-training days.

goals.name: Moderate-deficit days
goals.weeklyDeficitEstimate: ~900-2,100 cal/week = ~0.3-0.6 lb/week fat loss (within Helms 2014 0.5-1%/week lean-preserving range)
goals.rationale: Mon = post-Sunday-game recovery (no training). Thu = upper body lifting only (no lower-body demand). Sat = pre-Sunday-game prep (no training). Wed (heavy TB DL) and game days (Tue, Sun) stay at maintenance to fuel performance. Daily deficit of 300-500 cal below TDEE preserves lean mass per Helms 2014.
goals.evidence: Helms et al. 2014, JISSN (PMID 24864135): 0.5-1% BW/week loss rate for lean-mass preservation in resistance-trained athletes. Aragon et al. 2017 ISSN position stand (PMID 28630601): intermittent restriction not superior to daily small-deficit approach.

goals.name: Restaurant meal cap
goals.rationale: Hall 2019 ward study (Cell Metab) showed ultra-processed/restaurant food environments drive ~+500 cal/day spontaneous intake vs matched whole-food diets. Current pattern: 5-7 restaurant meals/week consistently drives 3,000+ cal days. Capping at 3 fixes the weekly caloric creep without daily macro tracking.

supersededGoals.name: Deficit rest days (original Apr 20 AM)
supersededGoals.supersededReason: Target was at BMR (~1,990 cal) which was more aggressive than needed. Math using rest-day TDEE (~2,400) showed actual deficit was only 300-400 cal per day = 600-800 cal/week, below the Helms 0.5%/week floor anyway. Revised to gentler 3-day approach.

dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Weak (athletes with adequate diet); see knowledge/supplements.md

dailySupplements.name: Fish oil
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Moderate for athletes; dose may be sub-therapeutic for tendon/recovery effects (2-3g EPA+DHA typical)

dailySupplements.name: Iron + vitamin C
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: CLINICALLY APPROPRIATE for Double Reds donor (2-3x/year). Red Cross recommends 18-38mg elemental for 120 days post-Power Red; his ~940-1,410mg/year iron loss justifies chronic supplementation. Dose is slightly above official 18-38mg range but within typical OTC tablet strength. Annual ferritin check warranted to confirm iron balance.

dailySupplements.name: Magnesium glycinate
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Moderate; appropriate dose and form for sleep/recovery

dailySupplements.name: Psyllium husk powder
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Strong for cholesterol, moderate for glycemic control and satiety. Jovanovski 2018 meta-analysis (Am J Clin Nutr, PMID 30239559): psyllium reduces LDL cholesterol 5-10% at 7-15g/day. 5g/day is modest; could benefit from 2-3x daily dosing or bump to 10g for full LDL effect per FDA qualified health claim threshold.

dailySupplements.name: Momentous Collagen Peptides (1 scoop)
dailySupplements.dose: 15g collagen peptides (11.6g grass-fed bovine + 5.2g FORTIGEL) + 50mg vitamin C, in shake
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Emerging for tendinopathy. Shaw 2017 (PMID 27852613) showed 15g gelatin/collagen + ~50mg vitamin C 30-60 min pre-loading doubles collagen synthesis biomarkers in serum. Praet 2019 (PMID 30609761) RCT used FORTIGEL specifically for achilles tendinopathy and found symptom improvements. Momentous formulation matches both protocols (15g + 50mg vit C, FORTIGEL inclusion). Optimal timing: 30-60 min PRE Wed/Fri HSR sessions for tendon-specific adaptation. Post-game/post-loading timing has weaker evidence but still contributes to general recovery.

activityLog.activity: Power Red (Double Red Cell) blood donation
activityLog.details: Apheresis donation, AM. Two units of packed red cells removed; plasma and platelets returned with saline. Logged as a physiological event for load context, not as a training or recovery session.
activityLog.notes: Power Red removes ~2 units of red cells (roughly double a whole-blood donation), lowering hemoglobin/hematocrit and oxygen-carrying capacity. The early hit has two clocks: a blood-volume loss that plasma re-expansion resolves in ~3 days, and a red-cell-mass deficit that takes weeks. Whole-blood RCTs show maximal power, VO2peak, and Hb mass still depressed up to ~4 weeks post-donation, with the sharpest acute VO2max drop (~15% at 2h, ~10% at 2d, ~7% at 7d) right after (Meurrens 2016 PMID 27873242; Hill, Vingren & Burdette 2013 doi:10.1139/apnm-2012-0361). Iron strongly speeds blood/Hb recovery (REDS-III, Kiss 2015 PMID 25668261) and, for a repeat donor like him, limits cumulative performance decline across donations (PMID 32931412); it does NOT fast-forward single-donation performance recovery, which returns in ~1 wk regardless. Full sourcing in knowledge/blood-donation-recovery.md. Because Power Red removes ~2x the red-cell mass, expect a longer return than the ~3-4 wk whole-blood window - the Red Cross 112-day Power Red deferral vs 56-day whole blood implies plan ~4-6+ weeks to baseline aerobic capacity. Iron loss is also ~2x. Evidence tier: Moderate (controlled performance data is whole-blood; the DRCA-specific timeline is extrapolated from those RCTs plus the regulatory deferral). Material because Phase 3 power-realization opens Wk 15 this week and 6/14 is a basketball-AM/hockey-PM double on a depleted system.

Sun AM basketball, 30 min high - first high-intensity session ~24h after the 6/13 Power Red donation, while plasma volume is still re-expanding (the largest acute aerobic hit is in the first ~48h). Intermittent court sport leans on anaerobic/skill more than a pure endurance test, so the practical impact is smaller than the ~10-15% VO2max drop seen on ergometer-to-exhaustion tests. PERCEIVED EXERTION (athlete report): wind noticeably down vs usual, but legs felt oddly decent - a clean aerobic-not-muscular dissociation, exactly the expected Power Red signal (red-cell-mass deficit hits O2 delivery, not the anaerobic ATP-PCr power system). No lightheadedness and no HR-won't-settle, so no donation red flags. Achilles reported fine (asymptomatic, logged 0/10) during and after. Sleep reported fine (hours not specified). PM hockey (22-25 min high) planned same day = a double-game day on reduced red-cell mass; will log after it happens.

PERCEIVED EXERTION (athlete report): wind quite difficult and legs felt pretty bad - a sharp flip from the AM read ('wind down, legs oddly decent'). The AM-to-PM reversal is the expected cumulative cost of a double on reduced red-cell mass: impaired O2 delivery slows recovery between the two sessions, and hockey's sustained-skating shifts tax the aerobic system harder than intermittent basketball. No lightheadedness reported - fatigue, not a donation red flag. Achilles not reported for the PM session (AM was 0/10). Sleep/protein for 6/14 not reported. Completed as prescribed - all loads held at the Phase 3 velocity-hold targets (TB DL 245, Back Squat 160), no PR push (Power Red week). NOTE: box jumps tonight + Fri plyo = jumps on back-to-back days plus tonight's heavy DL, on already-tight calves - watch the achilles Fri and trim plyo contacts if it speaks up (the ~13-week green streak is the program's anchor signal).

Sun AM basketball - INJURY EVENT, the headline of Wk 16. Early in the run, a minor pull/tightness in the RIGHT calf (graded minor, played through - likely a Grade I warning strain). A few minutes later, acute severe pain in the LEFT calf described as 'shot with a lacrosse ball' - the classic 'tennis leg' presentation of an acute medial gastrocnemius strain (Delgado 2002 PMID 12091669: medial gastroc ~67% of tennis-leg, plantaris only ~1.4%; mechanism = sudden knee extension with ankle dorsiflexion at push-off, Dixon 2009 PMID 19468870). Could not continue. Since (6/21-6/23): hard to bear weight and walk; resting, taking ibuprofen. Inability to weight-bear flags at least a Grade II strain and MANDATES ruling out the can't-miss differentials before any programming - Achilles rupture (Boyd 2015 PMID 26622031: rupture patients can still walk and weakly plantarflex, missed without the Thompson/Simmonds squeeze test) and DVT (~10% of tennis-leg, Delgado 2002; Wells 1997 PMID 9428249). Clinical-eval ask logged to clinicalWorkupAsks.acuteCalfInjuryJun2026. Full evidence + rehab plan in knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md.

activityLog.details: Left medial gastrocnemius strain, day 3. Protected weight-bearing, relative rest, elevation, ibuprofen for pain. No calf loading yet; pain-free ankle pumps / gentle dorsiflexion ROM only. No Tue basketball (out). PEACE phase (Dubois & Esculier 2020 PMID 31377722).
activityLog.notes: Phase 0 (acute/protected) per knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md. #1 action: in-person eval to rule out Achilles rupture (Thompson/Simmonds), assess DVT (Wells +/- Doppler), and grade the strain by ultrasound (aponeurotic involvement predicts a longer return - Green 2020 PMID 33030961). NSAID note (the athlete's question): short-course ibuprofen for pain is defensible - a 41-study meta-analysis found a small short-term benefit and no proven human harm (Morelli 2018 PMID 28355084); the PEACE 'avoid anti-inflammatories' line is precautionary about prolonged use and a theoretical bleeding/hematoma risk in the first 48-72h. Reasonable to transition to acetaminophen as pain eases (Rahusen 2004 PMID 15572312) and not run NSAIDs continuously through the multi-week healing phase. PROGRAM IMPACT: Phase 3 power block HALTED; HSR de-loaded to zero this week and rebuilt later, NOT continued at the prior 155 lb seated / BW+72 single-leg loads (de-load is extrapolated from muscle-healing biology + tendinopathy load management - Beyer 2015 PMID 26018970, Moderate transfer). Achilles green-zone streak is paused, not lost. SELF-SCREEN (later 6/23): no visible swelling or bruising, calf not lopsided vs the other leg, and the Thompson/Simmonds calf-squeeze test was normal and symmetric (foot plantarflexes on both sides). This makes a complete Achilles rupture much less likely (a clean symmetric squeeze test is strong reassurance; partial tears can still move, so not a 100% exclusion - Boyd 2015 PMID 26622031) and, with the symmetric non-swollen calf, lowers DVT probability (Wells 1997 PMID 9428249). Working diagnosis: acute medial gastrocnemius strain, likely Grade I-II. Proceeding with the Phase 0 to rehab block; a professional (physio/sports-med) grading assessment is now recommended but not urgent. Re-escalate if the calf later swells, warms, reddens, or turns one-sided, or if pain becomes disproportionate. Sleep/protein not reported for this window. Wk 16-18 rehab schedule written to modifiedWeeks.

Upper-body session kept the Phase 3 upper line alive while the lower block stays halted for the acute left medial gastroc strain (injured 6/21, day 4). Bench Press 4x4 at 160 lbs - first load above the 155 lb ceiling held since 5/28 (155 on 5/28, 6/10, and 6/18), a +5 lb top-end PR. DB Push Press held at 35 lb DBs (matching 6/18, up from 30 on 5/28). Seated Cable Row 3x10 ascending 130/135/140 - a higher-rep volume scheme vs the only prior cable-row entry (5/28, 5-rep sets topping 145). No calf/HSR work, correct for the rehab block. COACHING FLAG: 6/25 was prescribed as calf-rehab rest, and DB push press uses a leg dip-and-drive with loaded ankle plantarflexion that engages the gastroc - at day 4 of a Grade I-II medial gastroc strain that explosive plantarflexion drive is the one upper-day movement worth caution; strict overhead press (no leg drive) is the safer substitute until the calf clears isometrics. Sleep and calf pain during/after not yet reported - to be appended.

Calf-rehab-week upper day per the Wk 17 Wed plan - strict OH press subbed for DB push press to avoid loading ankle plantarflexion on the healing gastroc. Bench 4x5x160 - full prescribed reps at the 6/25 top-end weight (that session was 4x4x160), first clean session at this weight/rep target. Seated Calf Raise (HSR) 3x10x95 logged as the held rehab load for the week.

activityLog.details: Daily double-leg calf raises through the week per the Wk 17 rehab plan (Mon 6/29 first de-loaded HSR + isometrics, Tue 6/30 calf-sparing lower, Wed 7/1 HSR hold + upper, Fri 7/3 soleus + calf-sparing lower #2), progressing into single-leg calf raises entering Wk 18.
activityLog.notes: Athlete-reported end-of-week recall (exact per-day sets/reps/weights not captured): some pain during sessions but strength returning; stair descent pain improving versus the 6/29 review flag (previously sharp/unpredictable on specific steps). Exact per-day numeric pain scores (during / next-morning) were not logged, so the Silbernagel 24-hour return-to-baseline rule can't be precisely verified from this entry - trend is qualitatively positive.

Sun PM hockey - OFF-PLAN RETURN TO SPORT, the headline of this review. The Wk 18 plan had 7/5 as rest (no games; games explicitly gated on unmet RTS criteria). Played through with 'light' left-calf pain; tolerable, did not stop. This is ~day 14 post-injury (6/21) and objectively early Phase 2 (loaded strength) - hockey is a Phase 4 return-to-sport activity, so it skipped the intervening criteria gates: single-leg calf-raise >=30 reps at <=10% asymmetry, ~20 symptom-free single-leg hops, and symptom-free sport-specific COD/plyo exposure (Green 2022 PMID 35032233). Hockey's explosive push-off and change-of-direction are the strain's own mechanism (sudden knee extension + ankle dorsiflexion at push-off; Dixon 2009 PMID 19468870). Tolerating a full game with only light pain rather than a re-strain is genuinely reassuring - it suggests the strain was toward the milder end (Grade I-II low) and tissue tolerance is climbing - but it does NOT retroactively clear the objective gates, and recurrence risk is highest at 40 with a prior calf injury now on record, often 'without warning' (Green 2017 PMID 28259848; Green 2020 PMID 33030961; ~20% of recurrences occur before full recovery). MORNING-AFTER READ (7/6 AM, reported): light soreness on waking, already subsiding - low-grade and settling toward baseline within the 24-hour window (Silbernagel 2007 PMID 17307888). This PASSES the 24-hour rule and beats the 6/29 pattern of lingering morning-after pain: the tissue tolerated the hockey load. One game clearing the 24h rule is a go-ahead to keep building and continue cautious play - it is NOT full RTS clearance (single-leg capacity + asymmetry still untested). Exact duration, intensity, and numeric pain score not captured.

Phase 1 → Phase 2 transition checkpoint (Section 7).

STRENGTH (end of Phase 1): TB DL closes Phase 1 at 235x4x5 (+5 lb microload Apr 29 broke a 5-week 230 plateau — strongest single result of Phase 1). Back Squat 150x3x5, hit Phase 1 target. HSR COMPLIANCE: Week 8 hit Beyer 3x/week target for the first time in Phase 1 (Tue standing + Wed seated + Fri standing). Wk 6: 2/3, Wk 7: 1/3. The structural fix was the bonus Tuesday session — pattern worth carrying into Phase 2 weeks where Tue isn't a game day.

ACHILLES: 13-day all-green streak Apr 22 → May 1 across training, games, yardwork, sleep-poor nights, and a TB DL plateau-breaker. Strongest signal of the program that the Beyer 2015 HSR protocol is delivering. Sun May 3 game: not reported.

SLEEP: Apr 26 → Apr 30 hit 6 consecutive recovery nights (8h reported). Apr 30 → May 1 broke streak at 5.5h (single night, below the Section 2 reactive-deload trigger of 3+ consecutive). Friday plan correctly applied Knowles 2018 partial deload — volume -25%, intensity held, HSR full load.

VOLUME (Wk 8): Lower body 17-19 hard sets across Tue + Wed + Fri (mid-range of Section 3 6-10 sets/muscle/wk after sport contribution). Upper ~13 sets (in range). Calves 9 sets across 3 HSR sessions = on Beyer target.

BSS WK 8 WED FLAG: Apr 29 went 25 lb DBs x 5 reps (vs prescribed 20 DBs x 8) with lower-back discomfort — cumulative lumbar fatigue from TB DL + Back Squat + heavy unilateral. Resolves naturally at Phase 2: existing Phase 2 plan drops BSS from Wed and moves it to Fri at moderate load. No custom rule needed.

SAT FAT (continuing concern, longevity lens): Apr 26 → May 1 ran 5/6 days at or over the AHA 22 g/day ceiling (Apr 26 ~36g, Apr 28 ~22.5g, Apr 29 ~31g, Apr 30 ~41g, May 1 ~20.5g+ pre-dinner). Driver: cheese-forward dinners (parmesan, pizza, pepperoni). Lever: replace cheese-dominant dinner protein with leaner sources (JustBare strips, BLSS chicken, lean ground turkey) on 4-5 of 7 days.

PHASE 2 READINESS: All criteria met — strength progressing, achilles tolerant, HSR compliant, sport recovery good. Plyo introduction appropriate. Section 6 prescribes 40-60 contacts/session for non-specialized athletes; the existing Phase 2 Fri default lands at 30 contacts (Box Jump 3x4 + TB Jump Squat 3x3 + Broad Jump 3x3) — deliberately below floor for Wk 1 first plyo session in 8 weeks given bilateral achilles tendinopathy. scienceReviews.findings:
Wk 11 / Wk 3 of Phase 2 review (13 days since last review on 2026-05-04 — overdue by 6 days vs weekly cadence target).

STRENGTH PROGRESSION (Section 7 checkpoint — Wk 11 was the planned re-evaluation point):
- TB DL: 225 → 240 lb over 8 wks = 1.88 lb/wk = 8.1 lb/month. Inside Section 7 expected range (5-15 lb/month for months 0-6, intermediate). 145 lb remaining to target. At current pace, 385x5 lands ~18 months out; 12-month timeline plausible only if Phase 2 RPE 7-8 work accelerates from Phase 1 RPE 6-7.
- Back Squat: rebuilt from 45 lb pattern-work (Apr 8) to 155x4x5 (May 6). Real progression now ~5 lb/cycle. 160 lb to target. CLAUDE.md called 15-18 months more realistic than 12 for squat — current data supports that.
- Bench Press: 135 → 150 in 30 days = 3.5 lb/wk = 15.2 lb/month. Top of Section 7 range. 75 lb to target. Most realistic of the three; ~5-6 months at current pace.

HSR COMPLIANCE (the biggest miss this review):
- Wk 9: 2/3 sessions (Wed seated + Fri standing single-leg). Bonus Tue dropped vs Wk 8.
- Wk 10: 1/3 sessions (Fri only — Wed/Thu missed to work travel).
- Wk 11 to date: 0/3.
- 3 consecutive weeks below Beyer 2015 (PMID 26018970, Strong) protocol target. Wk 8 success was the bonus Tue HSR session; that pattern wasn't carried into Phase 2. Outcomes are protective so far (see Achilles), but compliance is the lagging indicator; Magnusson and Kjaer 2019 (PMC6395417, Strong) on multi-month tendon adaptation cycles argues against extended compliance gaps.

ACHILLES (the strongest signal in the program):
- 15-event green-zone (≤3/10) streak back to 2026-03-17. Zero events in yellow (4-5) or red (>5) range across past 30 days.
- Plyo introduction (5/9, 30 contacts) and ramp (5/15, 40 contacts) both completed at 0/10.
- Two plyo→game 48h clusters (Sat 5/9 → Sun 5/10; Fri 5/15 → Sun 5/17) both clean.
- Meets Section 4 criteria to continue Fri plyo block by wide margin.

PLYO BLOCK (Section 6):
- On schedule per 5/04 review forecast. Wk 9: 30 contacts (ramp floor). Wk 10: 40 contacts (+10 ramp, hitting Section 6 floor). Wk 11 prescribed 50, Wk 12 prescribed 60 (top of 40-60 range). After Wk 12 close, 2-week maintenance Wks 13-14 per Phase 3 plan.
- Markovic 2007 (Strong): ~4-8% VJ improvement from 6-12 weeks of plyo in trained athletes. The 4-week block targets the steeper portion of that curve.

VOLUME (Wk 10 anomaly):
- Wk 10 = 1 training session (Fri plyo) vs typical 3, due to work travel. Net: zero direct upper-body sets, zero non-plyo lower-body sets.
- Functionally an unscheduled reactive 'deload.' Per Coleman 2024 (PMID 38274324, Moderate), unscheduled deloads without fatigue signals can cost strength gain — but Wk 10 was sport/work-driven not fatigue-driven, and adjacent outcomes (achilles, game performance) were intact. Treat as one-off, not a pattern.

RECOVERY DATA GAP:
- Sleep logged 4/14 nights (29%) since last review.
- Protein logged 3/14 days (21%).
- Body comp: 1 reading (5/7: 220.5 lb / 23.0% BF, essentially flat from 5/04 baseline of 221.3 / 23.2%).
- Per CLAUDE.md: 'Data gaps on these two fields compromise the 12-week HSR adaptation check and the Knowles 2018 sleep-intensity auto-regulation.' Now a compounded gap across two consecutive reviews (Wk 6 review also flagged this).

PHASE 2 STATUS:
- Wk 11 = Wk 3 of Phase 2. RPE targets shifted from 6-7 (Phase 1) to 7-8 (Phase 2) — too early to tell if working-set RPE actually moved; the Wk 11 + Wk 13 sessions will be the read.
- 4 weeks until Phase 3 entry (Wk 15, mid-June). Phase 2 plyo block runs Wks 9-12; maintenance Wks 13-14; Phase 3 begins 'power realization' work.
scienceReviews.changes:

2) RECOVERY CAPTURE TRIGGER: Standardize a single morning log: sleep hours / AM achilles 0-10 / protein floor estimate (Y/N hit 40g by lunch). Target 80% capture (≥5/7 days/week). The Silbernagel 24-hour rule and Knowles 2018 sleep-intensity auto-regulation both depend on consistent daily reads. References: Silbernagel 2007 (PMID 17307888, Strong); Knowles 2018 (PMID 29422383, Moderate); Walsh 2021 IOC consensus (PMID 33144349, Strong).

3) HOLD 12-MONTH STRENGTH TARGETS THROUGH WK 14: Re-evaluate after Phase 2 plyo block closes (5/29) and 2-week maintenance Wks 13-14 complete. Current progression rates (TB DL 8.1 lb/mo, Bench 15.2 lb/mo) are inside Section 7 expected ranges; Back Squat will be the constraint and likely confirms the 15-18 month timeline CLAUDE.md flagged. Hold for now. References: Rhea 2003 dose-response meta-analysis (Moderate); Baker 2001 (PMID 11710401, Moderate) on in-season constraint.

scienceReviews.findings:
Wk 12 end-of-week / plyo-block-close review (13 days since the 2026-05-17 review; the 5/17 entry named today's checkpoint - re-evaluate Section 7 targets after the block closes 5/29). Window covered: 5/18 (Wk 11 back-half) through 5/29 (Wk 12 close).

STRENGTH (Section 7 re-evaluation - the purpose of this checkpoint):
- TB DL: held 240 for a 2nd clean session 5/20 (a 14-day layoff did NOT produce the forecast RPE bump - pattern intact), then +5 to 245 on 5/27 (clean, first compound +5 in 4 weeks). Now 140 lb from the 385x5 target. Rate is still ~8 lb/month (Wk 11 review figure); the Phase 2 RPE 7-8 shift has not yet accelerated TB DL beyond its Phase 1 rate. At this pace 385x5 lands ~17-18 months out, NOT 12. Honest read: TB DL 385 is an 18-month target.
- Bench: held 150 for a 2nd clean session 5/21, then +5 to 155 on 5/28 (clean, RPE 7-8). Now 70 lb from 225x5 at ~15 lb/month (Wk 11 figure) = ~4-5 months. On pace for the 12-month target and arguably ahead. Most reliable of the three.
- Back Squat: held 155 (5/20, 2nd clean session, opened 160) but the 160 push was DEFERRED on 5/27 - no squat rack available, substituted Leg Press 3x10 (90/180/270). So an earned +5 is un-cashed and the constraint lift lost a progression to equipment, not to the athlete. 160 lb from 315x5; CLAUDE.md's 15-18 month call is confirmed by the data. Equipment availability is now a live risk for the squat specifically.
Section 7 conclusion: Bench 225 holds at 12 months (tracking ahead); TB DL 385 and Squat 315 are honestly 18-month targets at observed rates. Recommend re-horizoning rather than carrying targets the data won't support (Rhea 2003 dose-response, Moderate; Baker 2001 PMID 11710401 in-season constraint; Lexell 1995 age recovery factor).

HSR COMPLIANCE (Beyer 2015 PMID 26018970, Strong - the headline reversal):
- Wk 11: 3/3 (Wed seated + Wed single-leg + Fri single-leg). First 3/3 since Wk 8.
- Wk 12: 3/3 (Wed single-leg + Fri seated + Fri single-leg double-variant make-up).
- TWO consecutive Beyer-compliant weeks, reversing the Wk 9-11 decline (2/3 -> 1/3 -> 0/3-at-last-review). The 5/17 structural fix (restore Seated Calf to Wed) is the direct driver.
- CAVEAT: both weeks were recovered by reactive make-up, not the prescribed Mon-AM HSR mini-session. The Monday anchor (the 5/17 review's #1 fix) was NOT adopted either week (5/18 = beach walk instead, 5/25 = rest). Friday double-up works but is fragile - one forgotten Friday = a missed week. Durability still depends on the unrealized Monday anchor. scienceReviews.findings:
ACUTE INJURY REVIEW (off the weekly cadence - triggered by the Sun 6/21 calf injury, not the calendar; 8 days since the 6/15 Wk 15 review).

WHAT HAPPENED: Sun 6/21 AM basketball. A minor RIGHT-calf pull (played through - likely Grade I) preceded, by minutes, an acute severe LEFT-calf injury described as 'shot with a lacrosse ball.' That description plus difficulty bearing weight since is the textbook presentation of an acute medial gastrocnemius strain ('tennis leg') - the medial gastroc accounts for ~67% of these, plantaris only ~1.4% (Delgado 2002 PMID 12091669, Strong); mechanism is sudden knee extension with the ankle dorsiflexed at push-off, which maximally stretches the biarticular gastroc (Dixon 2009 PMID 19468870, Strong). The right calf was likely a Grade I warning that the athlete pushed past - playing through the first calf is a plausible contributor to the second.

SEVERITY / DIFFERENTIAL: Inability to weight-bear puts this at AT LEAST Grade II. Before any programming, the can't-miss differentials must be cleared in person (logged to clinicalWorkupAsks.acuteCalfInjuryJun2026): (1) Achilles rupture - the critical miss, because rupture patients can still walk and weakly plantarflex and may have no palpable gap, so it is missed unless the Thompson/Simmonds calf-squeeze test is done (Boyd 2015 PMID 26622031, Strong; Maffulli 1998 PMID 9548122 squeeze-test sensitivity 0.96); (2) DVT - present in ~10% of tennis-leg presentations (Delgado 2002; Wells 1997 PMID 9428249, Strong); (3) compartment syndrome if pain is out of proportion with a tense compartment (emergency). This is a coaching read, not a diagnosis.

PROGRAM STATE AT INJURY: Phase 3 (power realization) Wk 2, opening on a Power-Red-depleted system, achilles green ~13 weeks, HSR bolt-on fix holding 3/3. The injury supersedes all of it. The Wk 16 'scheduled deload, lean toward honoring it' note from the 6/15 review is now moot - this is an injury rehab block, not a deload.

ACUTE MANAGEMENT / NSAID QUESTION: PEACE then LOVE (Dubois & Esculier 2020 PMID 31377722, Moderate-as-guidance). On the athlete's ibuprofen question: short-course NSAID for pain is defensible and not clearly harmful short-term - a 41-study meta-analysis found a small short-term benefit, no proven human harm (Morelli 2018 PMID 28355084, Moderate-to-Strong); the mechanistic 'NSAIDs blunt satellite cells' concern is from exercise-damage models and is contradicted by a large-injury study where ibuprofen HELPED (Mikkelsen 2009 PMID 19713429 vs Mackey 2016 PMID 26936358). Net: use ibuprofen short-term for pain, transition to acetaminophen as pain eases (Rahusen 2004 PMID 15572312), do not run NSAIDs continuously for weeks; mind a theoretical bleeding/hematoma risk in the first 48-72h. Early protected loading beats immobilization (Jarvinen 2005 PMID 15851777; Bayer 2017 PMID 28953439, day-2 vs day-9 rehab returned athletes ~3 wks sooner with no reinjury increase).

RECURRENCE CONTEXT (why conservative is correct): calf strains recur frequently and 'without warning'; the two strongest risk factors are increasing AGE and PREVIOUS calf injury (Green 2017 PMID 28259848, Strong), and older age + prior injury predict EARLY recurrence (Green 2020 PMID 33030961). At 40 and now with a calf event on record, this athlete is in the highest-risk band - the single strongest argument for finishing the full criteria-based progression rather than returning on feel.

Created knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md (verified citations, evidence tiers, can't-miss differentials, PEACE & LOVE, NSAID verdict, phased rehab, RTS criteria, recurrence) and added it to the knowledge README index. The objective RTS gates are UNTESTED - single-leg calf-raise capacity (target >=30 reps) and inter-limb asymmetry (target <=10%), ~20 symptom-free single-leg hops, hop asymmetry (Green 2022; Hebert-Losier 2017 PMID 28886865). Calf strains recur frequently and 'without warning,' and his exact band - age 40 + a prior calf injury now on record - is the highest-risk band, with ~20% of recurrences occurring before full recovery (Green 2017 PMID 28259848; Green 2020). 'Feels fine' is precisely the signal the literature says fails silently. A residual strength/capacity deficit can persist while pain is gone, and that deficit is the recurrence substrate.

SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT MISSING DATUM: the 7/6 morning-after read - did the calf settle to baseline within 24h of the hockey game (Silbernagel 2007 PMID 17307888 24-hour rule). Not yet reported. Applied to a game, this is now the highest-value early-warning signal available and it directly gates whether continued play is defensible.

UPPER (the intact line): Bench 4x5x160 on 7/1 - full prescribed reps at the weight that was a 4-rep top-end PR on 6/25. One more clean 4x5x160 earns +5 to 165 under the standard double-progression rule (2 clean sessions -> load up; no fractional plates, so +5). This line has been preserved cleanly through the whole injury.

DATA GAPS (chronic, still compounding): morning-after pain read is now the #1 gap (post-game and post-session); single-leg capacity + asymmetry numbers are UNTESTED (the one objective number that would tell us if play is safe); body comp no reading since 6/12 (24 days, 219.5 lb / 22.7% BF); protein 0% logged since 5/11 (~8 weeks); sleep sporadic.

NUTRITION / RECOMP: the 6/29 'hold calories toward the rest-day end' flag PARTIALLY REVERSES - with hockey back (and presumably more sport to come), TDEE is climbing off the layoff floor, so the fat-regain-during-inactivity risk eases and calories should re-anchor to activity, not stay suppressed. No intake data to confirm either way - flag only. Protein floor unchanged.

ACHILLES (prior anchor): tendon remains the non-problem; the gastroc strain is the active issue. Green-zone streak still paused, not lost; resumes as a tracked signal once the strain clears.
scienceReviews.changes:
1) MEET REALITY - REFRAME FROM LINEAR REHAB TO CONFIRM-AND-PROTECT. He is back to sport and it went okay; coaching him as if he is still in protected rehab is fiction. The job now is to confirm the tissue is actually ready and shore up the residual deficit, not to pretend the game did not happen. Autonomy respected - games are the A-priority - but the return is ahead of the evidence-based gates and that is stated plainly, not softened.

2) GET THE OBJECTIVE NUMBER THIS WEEK (the single highest-leverage action). Single-leg calf-raise to fatigue, BOTH legs, floor range, controlled tempo: record reps each side and % asymmetry vs the uninjured leg (RTS targets >=30 reps, <=10% asymmetry - Green 2022; Hebert-Losier 2017 PMID 28886865). This is now DIAGNOSTIC of whether continued play is safe, not merely a rehab milestone. Everything to date is qualitative; this is the first hard number. LOG IT.

3) THE MORNING-AFTER READ IS THE GOVERNOR - NOW APPLIED TO GAMES. After every game and every hard session, log pain-during (0-10), pain-next-morning (0-10), and whether it settled to baseline in 24h (Silbernagel 2007 PMID 17307888). A game that leaves the calf WORSE the next morning is the pull-back signal - drop back to loaded rehab that week. Start with the 7/6 read on last night's hockey.

4) KEEP LOADING CALF STRENGTH - THIS IS THE ACTIVE PROTECTION, MORE THAN REST. Continue HSR rebuild toward (not past) prior loads, plus single-leg (knee-straight, gastroc) and bent-knee (soleus) raises, to close any inter-limb asymmetry. A capacity/strength deficit is the recurrence substrate; loading it out is what actually lowers reinjury risk, not backing off. Pain rule throughout: <=5/10 during and next-morning, settle in 24h, no week-to-week increase (bias conservative given the bilateral Achilles-tendinopathy context).

5) IF PLAYING, DE-RISK EACH GAME. Thorough calf warm-up (raises + graded push-off) before the game; ease into maximal push-off/COD rather than cold; treat any sharp, sudden calf pain as an immediate stop. Recurrence in his band is frequent and often without warning (Green 2017/2020) - the warm-up and the stop-on-sharp-pain rule are cheap insurance.

6) NUTRITION - RELEASE THE REST-DAY CALORIE HOLD. With games resuming, re-anchor calories to activity days (~2,400-2,600 on game/training days) rather than the 6/29 layoff-protective ~2,100-2,300. Keep the protein floor at ~160-220 g/day for healing tissue (Morton 2018 PMID 28698222, Strong). Flag only - no intake logged. A body-comp reading would resolve whether the 22.7% program-low (6/12) held through the layoff.

OPEN / CARRIED FORWARD: clinical grading eval status unconfirmed - if not yet seen it is still worth doing for a definitive grade, but urgency is lower now that sport tolerance is demonstrated (self-screen 6/23 + hockey 7/5); re-escalate urgently only on new unilateral swelling/warmth/redness (DVT) or pain disproportionate to exam (Wells 1997 PMID 9428249). Section 7 target re-horizon (hold Bench 225 at 12mo; move TB DL 385 / Squat 315 to ~18mo) STILL PENDING YOUR SIGN-OFF - flagged 5/30, 6/15, 6/29; the layoff makes the lower-body 18-month framing more realistic, worth closing soon. Lower-body barbell lifts (TB DL, squat) remain frozen pending calf clearance - reintroduce only after the capacity/asymmetry gate and a clean morning-after pattern.

ADDENDUM (7/6, morning-after read received): last night's hockey left only light soreness on waking that is already subsiding - PASSES the Silbernagel 24-hour rule and beats the 6/29 lingering pattern, so the tissue tolerated the game. Verdict moves from conditional to a confirmed go-ahead to keep building and continue cautious play; the single-leg capacity/asymmetry number (change #2) remains the gating unknown before treating full sport as cleared, and the per-game morning-after read (change #3) stays the running governor.

NEXT REVIEW: end of Wk 18 (Sun 7/12), or sooner if the morning-after pain rises after a game or any red flag appears.

15.label: Phase 3 Wk 1 (power realization). VJ baseline still deferred - the Wed upper-power session shifted to Thu AM and the VJ was skipped (4th+ deferral); targeting the Fri plyo warm-up, else the Wk 16 deload. Goal #3 still has zero data. Power intent = velocity and contrast/PAP at HELD loads (TB DL 245 / Squat 160 / Bench 155), not heavier; ~40-contact plyo maintained; HSR bolted onto all 3 lift days (Wed/Thu/Fri = 3/3, the proven Wk 14 fix). Calibrated to the 6/13 Power Red: aerobic capacity down ~4-6 wks, so power (spared) is the focus, conditioning and PRs are out, rest periods long. Wk 16 = scheduled deload - lean toward honoring it.
15.reason: First Phase 3 week, opening on the system left depleted by the 6/13 Power Red and the 6/14 basketball-AM + hockey-PM double. Phase 2 closed ready (strength held through the maintenance bridge, achilles green ~13 wks, the HSR bolt-on fix hit 3/3 in Wk 14, body comp broke its plateau to 219.5/22.7%), so power realization is the right next block. But the donation reshapes HOW it opens. Power (ATP-PCr) is the quality least affected by reduced red-cell mass, while aerobic capacity and between-effort recovery are down for ~4-6+ weeks (Meurrens 2016 PMID 27873242; Kiss 2015 PMID 25668261; knowledge/blood-donation-recovery.md). So Wk 15: (1) VJ BASELINE goes first on Wed, on the freshest legs, before any lifting - it has been deferred 3+ times and Phase 3 is un-evaluable against goal #3 without it (Markovic 2007, Strong); a power baseline is valid even on a depleted week since power is spared. (2) HOLD all loads and express them fast (velocity / compensatory acceleration) rather than adding weight - no PR chase on a depleted system (Baker 2001 PMID 11710401, Moderate). (3) Lower power via contrast/complex - heavy submaximal lift paired with an explosive jump for post-activation potentiation (Suchomel 2016; Markovic 2007). (4) Plyo held at ~40 contacts Fri - a realization/maintenance dose, down from the 60-contact Phase 2 peak (Ebben 2010). (5) HSR bolted onto Wed/Thu/Fri (Beyer 2015 PMID 26018970, Strong) - the durability fix that worked. (6) Long rest (3-5 min) between power sets and NO conditioning finishers for ~4-6 weeks. Auto-regulate everything aerobic by RPE (reads 1-2 points high). STANDING GAPS: protein 0% logged since 5/11 (one daily read: 40g-by-lunch Y/N); strength-target re-horizon still pending athlete sign-off (targets unchanged at TB DL 385 / Squat 315 / Bench 225). Wk 16 is the next scheduled deload - lean toward honoring it given the donation's systemic recovery demand.

16.title: INJURED - acute calf strain (Phase 0 protection)
16.subtitle: Left medial gastrocnemius strain Sun 6/21 ('shot in the calf'). Phase 3 is HALTED. This week = protected weight-bearing + an in-person clinical eval. No games, no calf loading. Full plan: knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md.
16.reason: Sun 6/21 AM basketball: a minor right-calf pull was played through, then minutes later an acute severe left-calf strain - the textbook 'tennis leg' (acute medial gastrocnemius strain; Delgado 2002 PMID 12091669; Dixon 2009 PMID 19468870). Difficulty bearing weight since = at least Grade II. The #1 priority is an IN-PERSON exam before any programming: rule out Achilles rupture via the Thompson/Simmonds squeeze test (the critical miss - rupture patients can still walk and weakly plantarflex; Boyd 2015 PMID 26622031; Maffulli 1998 PMID 9548122), assess DVT (~10% of tennis-leg; Wells 1997 PMID 9428249), and seek emergency care if pain is out of proportion with a tense compartment. Acute care = PEACE then LOVE (Dubois & Esculier 2020 PMID 31377722): protect, elevate, compression, education; short-course ibuprofen for pain is fine (Morelli 2018 PMID 28355084) but transition to acetaminophen as pain eases and don't run NSAIDs for weeks. Early protected loading beats immobilization (Jarvinen 2005 PMID 15851777; Bayer 2017 PMID 28953439), so gentle pain-free ROM starts now and pain-free isometrics late this week. HSR is de-loaded to zero and rebuilt later - NOT continued at the pre-injury 155 lb / BW+72 loads. Recurrence risk is high (age + prior calf injury are the top risk factors; Green 2017 PMID 28259848), so the whole block is criteria-gated.

17.reason: Phase 1 begins once the athlete walks without a limp, isometrics are pain-free, and the can't-miss differentials are excluded. Progress isometrics -> double-leg -> single-leg calf raises through a tolerable range, and REINTRODUCE HSR at a markedly reduced load (rebuild, do not resume the pre-injury 155 lb seated / BW+72 single-leg loads). The rehab trajectory and the Achilles tendinopathy plan are the same progressive-loading trajectory, so they reconverge here (Beyer 2015 PMID 26018970 as the loading substrate; transfer to acute strain is Moderate/extrapolated). Pain rule throughout (Silbernagel 2007 PMID 17307888): <=5/10 during/next-morning, no week-to-week increase; bias conservative. No games this week - calf strains recur 'without warning' and the priority order is injury prevention first. | 6/29 REVIEW UPDATE: calf is improving but at the LOAD CEILING - morning-after pain lingers (the 24h return-to-baseline rule is not cleanly met) and stair descent plus occasional sharp steps still hurt (eccentric gastroc reactivity). HOLD rehab load this week; gate any increase (the Wed/Fri steps, single-leg progression, soleus loading) on the next-morning pain FIRST returning to baseline. WHAT YOU CAN STILL TRAIN to limit detraining: upper body (as the 6/25 session) plus calf-SPARING lower isolation - seated leg extension, hip thrust / glute bridge, back extension (bent knee + quiet ankle = minimal gastroc demand). HOLD all calf-loading compounds until Phase 2: back squat, deadlift incl. trap bar, leg press, lunges / split squats, heavy leg curls, and all jumping / running. The gastroc is biarticular - a loaded squat stretches it at depth and a deadlift loads it under heavy axial drive, which is too much at ~day 8 of a Grade I-II strain. Recurrence risk is highest at 40 with a calf injury now on record (Green 2017 PMID 28259848; Green 2020 PMID 33030961) - do not load on feel.

schedule.desc: Calf rehab (HOLD load) + UPPER. Calf: raises + de-loaded HSR at Mon's load (no step up). Upper: bench, strict OH press, row, pulldown + core.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.notes: step up only after a clean morning (24h rule). ROM: knee-bent, full plantarflexion, controlled to a pain-free bottom; no deep deficit stretch yet

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.notes: after the 6/25 4x4x160 top-end PR

exercises.name: Strict Overhead Press
exercises.notes: STRICT - no leg dip/drive (subs for DB push press, which loads ankle plantarflexion)

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.notes: core, calf-neutral

schedule.longDesc: Upper day with calf rehab bolted on at HELD load. Strict overhead press replaces DB push press - the push-press dip-and-drive loads ankle plantarflexion (the 6/25 flag) on a fresh gastroc strain. Rebuild HSR load only once a morning settles to baseline (Silbernagel 2007).

schedule.desc: Recovery: relative rest for the calf (ROM + isometrics). Optional core + upper-body bike for blood flow. Re-assess morning-after calf response.
schedule.longDesc: Phase 1 exit criteria: pain-free full active ROM, single-leg calf-raise capacity building, no morning-after escalation. Vascularisation (LOVE): a pain-free upper-body ergometer drives blood flow without loading the gastroc. Keep this day genuinely light - the prior days carried work.

schedule.desc: Calf rehab (soleus) + LOWER (calf-sparing) #2. Calf: double-leg + light bent-knee soleus raises + de-loaded HSR at held load. Legs: leg extension, hip thrust, hip abduction + core.

exercises.name: Double-leg calf raise
exercises.notes: still HOLD load unless mornings have gone clean. ROM: FLOOR RANGE, no heel-drop deficit off a step yet

exercises.name: Bent-knee (soleus) calf raise
exercises.notes: bent knee slackens the gastroc and biases soleus - lower risk to the strained medial gastroc; keep light + pain-free. ROM: controlled range, no forced bottom stretch yet

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.notes: ROM: knee-bent, full plantarflexion, controlled to a pain-free bottom; no deep deficit stretch yet

exercises.name: Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: quads

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: glutes, heel-driven

exercises.name: Hip Abduction (machine)
exercises.notes: hip stability, calf-neutral

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: anti-extension core

schedule.longDesc: Second calf-sparing leg session plus the soleus (bent-knee) introduction from the base Phase 1 plan, kept light. Soleus work matters because it is not loaded by knee-straight raises (Dixon 2009 PMID 19468870); the bent knee also protects the strained gastroc. Keep all calf work pain-free at the held load until a morning wakes at baseline.

schedule.desc: Rest (July 4). Optional easy pain-free walk / upper-body aerobic. Gate into Wk 18 on pain-free full ROM + building single-leg capacity + clean mornings.
schedule.longDesc: Criteria, not calendar. Hold in Phase 1 longer if morning-after symptoms persist. Wk 18 = progressive loaded strength incl. soleus once Phase 1 exit criteria are met.

18.title: Calf: Phase 2 strength + monitored return to games
18.subtitle: Hockey 7/5 tolerated (light morning-after soreness that subsided = passed the 24h rule), so games are back on WITH de-risking - but the return ran ahead of the objective gates. Confirm-and-protect this week: MEASURE the single-leg capacity/asymmetry number, keep LOADING calf strength (gastroc + soleus), MONITOR the morning-after read after every game as the governor. Lower barbell lifts stay frozen until the capacity gate clears. knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md.
18.reason: Wk 18 pivots from linear rehab to CONFIRM-AND-PROTECT after the athlete returned to hockey off-plan on Sun 7/5 (day 14) and tolerated it - light morning-after soreness that subsided within the 24-hour window (Silbernagel 2007 PMID 17307888), a pass that beats the 6/29 lingering pattern. That clears the gate the prior review waited on, so cautious continued play is defensible and games are the A-priority (no longer withheld). BUT the return jumped ahead of the objective RTS gates, which remain UNTESTED: single-leg calf-raise capacity >=30 reps and inter-limb asymmetry <=10%, plus loaded strength toward >=1.0x BW knee-extended and >=1.5x BW knee-flexed soleus (Green 2022 PMID 35032233; Hebert-Losier 2017 PMID 28886865). A capacity/strength deficit can persist while pain is gone, and that deficit is the recurrence substrate - recurrence is frequent, 'without warning,' and highest in his band (age 40 + prior calf injury; Green 2017 PMID 28259848; Green 2020 PMID 33030961). So this week does three things at once: (1) MEASURE - get the single-leg capacity/asymmetry number early, fresh (the one hard datum still missing); (2) LOAD - keep building calf strength, gastroc (knee-straight) + soleus (bent-knee) HSR rebuilding toward but not past the pre-injury loads, because loading out the deficit is the active protection against recurrence, more than rest is; (3) MONITOR - log pain-during and pain-next-morning after every game/hard session; light-and-subsiding = keep going, lingering-or-climbing = pull back to loaded rehab that week. De-risk each game: thorough calf warm-up, ease into maximal push-off, stop on any sharp calf pain. Lower-body BARBELL lifts (TB DL, squat) stay FROZEN until the capacity gate clears and mornings stay clean. Pain rule throughout: <=5/10 during and next-morning, settle in 24h, no week-to-week increase; bias conservative given the bilateral Achilles-tendinopathy context. Full evidence: knowledge/calf-strain-rehab.md.

schedule.desc: Hockey PM - OFF-PLAN return to sport (day 14). Tolerated, played through light calf pain; 7/6 morning-after soreness light and subsiding = PASSED the Silbernagel 24-hour rule.
schedule.longDesc: This is the clearance signal the rest of the week is built on - ONE game tolerated, not a full RTS clearance. The plan had this as rest; it went okay and reframed the week to confirm-and-protect.

schedule.desc: CAPACITY TEST (do first, while fresh) + loaded calf strength. Single-leg calf-raise to fatigue both legs = the week's key number. Then loaded raises + soleus + HSR rebuild.

exercises.name: Single-leg calf-raise to fatigue
exercises.notes: the week's key datum - record reps + % asymmetry vs the uninjured leg (RTS target >=30 reps, <=10% gap). Test FRESH, not post-game. ROM: floor range, full plantarflexion up, controlled down to neutral; no heel-drop deficit off a step yet (stair descent only 'improving', not pain-free)

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.notes: rebuild toward, not past, pre-injury (seated was 95 lb on 7/1). ROM: knee-bent/soleus-biased, full plantarflexion, controlled to a pain-free bottom; no deep dorsiflexion stretch yet

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise (HSR, standing)
exercises.notes: gastroc bias. ROM: floor range only

exercises.name: Bent-knee (soleus) calf raise
exercises.notes: bent knee slackens gastroc, biases soleus. ROM: controlled, no forced bottom stretch

schedule.longDesc: Heavy-slow tempo (3s up / 3s down) as load allows. Pain <=5/10 during and next-morning, settle in 24h. The capacity test is now DIAGNOSTIC of whether continued play is safe, not just a milestone - log the numbers.

schedule.desc: Basketball PM - CLEARED WITH CAUTION (hockey was tolerated). De-risk: thorough calf warm-up + graded push-off, ease in, STOP on any sharp calf pain.

exercises.name: Pre-game calf warm-up
exercises.notes: do not go cold into max push-off

schedule.longDesc: Games are A-priority and hockey passed the 24h rule, so basketball is defensible - but it is still ahead of the objective gate. If the Mon capacity test didn't happen, do it Tue AM pre-game (fresh, not post-game). Log the Wed-AM morning-after read - light+subsiding = keep going, lingering/climbing = pull back to loaded rehab this week.

schedule.desc: Upper day (the intact line) + calf strength. Bench toward 225, strict OH press, row, pulldown; plus HSR + loaded calf raises + kinetic-chain glute/ham.

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.notes: double progression - 7/1 was the first clean 4x5x160

exercises.name: Strict Overhead Press
exercises.notes: STRICT - no leg dip/drive (spares ankle plantarflexion)

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.weight: held load; +step only after clean mornings
exercises.notes: ROM: knee-bent, full plantarflexion, controlled pain-free bottom

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.notes: ROM: floor range

exercises.name: Glute-Ham / Hip work
exercises.notes: proximal chain contributes to plantarflexion force; calf-sparing

schedule.longDesc: Upper line preserved cleanly through the injury - keep it progressing. Rebuild HSR load only once mornings stay clean (Silbernagel 24h rule).

schedule.desc: Relative rest + re-assess. Review the capacity/asymmetry number and the week's morning-after reads against the RTS benchmarks. Optional pain-free upper-body bike.
schedule.longDesc: Decision point: capacity climbing toward >=30 reps at <=10% asymmetry + clean mornings after games = green to keep building and playing. Deficit or lingering mornings = hold load, keep games conservative.

schedule.desc: Heaviest calf load of the week (still <= pre-injury): HSR + single-leg (gastroc) + bent-knee (soleus) raises + calf-sparing lower isolation.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.notes: ROM: knee-bent, full plantarflexion, pain-free bottom

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise (HSR)
exercises.notes: ROM: floor range; earn the heel-drop deficit only once stair descent is fully pain-free

exercises.name: Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: quads, calf-neutral

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: glutes, heel-driven, calf-sparing

schedule.longDesc: Keep it pain-free; mornings clean before any step-up. Lower-body BARBELL lifts (TB DL, squat) stay frozen until the capacity gate clears + mornings stay clean.

schedule.desc: Rest. Next week gated on the capacity/asymmetry number + a clean morning-after pattern across the week's games.
schedule.longDesc: Barbell lower lifts reintroduce only after the capacity gate clears. Phase 3 (running/hopping/plyo prep) opens once single-leg capacity is climbing and ~20 symptom-free single-leg hops are met - written week-by-week, not pre-scheduled.
